raw_data.details: The Post-Doctoral Fellowship (PDF) at the Physical Research Laboratory (PRL) is an initiative to support researchers who have completed or are nearing the completion of their Ph. D. in science or engineering fields. The fellowship offers candidates the opportunity to pursue research in PRL’s cutting-edge science and engineering research areas. The tenure of the fellowship is for a maximum of two years, subject to annual progress reviews. The fellowship provides a competitive stipend, research opportunities, and accommodation benefits.
raw_data.benefits:
Fellowship: Monthly stipend based on the academic status of the fellow:₹58,000 for candidates who have submitted their Ph. D. thesis but await the degree.
₹61,000 for candidates holding a Ph. D. degree.
₹67,000 for external applicants with a Ph. D. and at least two years of research experience. Academic Allowance: One-time academic allowance of ₹10,000 during the fellowship. Accommodation: Fellows may receive PRL accommodation or HRA in lieu of accommodation as per PRL norms.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should complete a Bachelor’s and master’s degrees in science or engineering with first-class (60%) or equivalent grades at both levels. The applicant’s age should be 35 years.
raw_data.application_process:
Online
Step 1: Eligible candidates are requested to apply via the ONLINE application form provided below.
Step 2: At the time of the interview, the candidates are required to produce original documents of the following: No Objection Certificate from the employer concerned in respect of those applicants who are employed under Central / State Government / Public Sector Undertakings / Autonomous Bodies. Links:[Click here to apply online](https://zfrmz.in/0mYXO8v2lD2Q3lNRlKi6) (All External Applicants to apply via this form ONLY)[Application forms for PRL SRFs to apply for a PDF fellowship](https://www.prl.res.in/prl-eng/sites/default/files/documents/research-program-doc/Application-forms-for-PRL-SRFS-to-apply-for-a-PDF-fellowship.doc)[Application forms for applying for an extension after one year of tenure](https://www.prl.res.in/prl-eng/sites/default/files/documents/research-program-doc/Application-forms-for-applying-for-extension-after-one-year-tenure.doc)[Form for submitting the final report](https://www.prl.res.in/prl-eng/sites/default/files/documents/research-program-doc/Form-for-submitting-the-final-report-2023.doc)
raw_data.documents_required: Passport-size Photograph. Ph. D. Thesis Abstract. List of Publications. Research Proposal (maximum of 2000 words). Detailed Bio-data (details of educational qualifications, age, work experience). Recommendation Letters from Referees. No Objection Certificate (for employed candidates).

Ph. D. Thesis Abstract. List of Publications. Research Proposal (maximum of 2000 words). Detailed Bio-data (details of educational qualifications, age, work experience). Recommendation Letters from Referees. No Objection Certificate (for employed candidates). Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the duration of the Post-Doctoral Fellowship at PRL? The fellowship is for a maximum of two years, with an annual review for extension.

What is the stipend offered under the fellowship? The stipend ranges from ₹58,000 to ₹67,000 per month, depending on the applicant’s qualifications.

Can candidates who have submitted their Ph. D. thesis apply for the fellowship? Yes, candidates who have submitted their Ph. D. thesis are eligible. Is there an age limit for applying to the fellowship? Yes, the age limit is 35 years. What fields of research are covered under this fellowship? PRL covers various fields such as Astronomy, Physics, Geosciences, Space Sciences, and Theoretical Physics. Is it mandatory to submit a research proposal? Yes, candidates must submit a research proposal in consultation with a PRL faculty member.

How many recommendation letters are required for the application? At least three recommendation letters from referees who are not PRL faculty members are required. Are there specific deadlines for application submission? Yes, applications are accepted quarterly, with deadlines in November, February, May, and August.

Is the fellowship open to foreign nationals? No, only Indian citizens are eligible to apply.

Can employed candidates apply for the fellowship? Yes, but they must provide a No Objection Certificate from their employer.

What is the selection process for the fellowship? Selection is based on an initial screening, followed by an interview. When will selected candidates be notified? Notifications are sent after the interview process. Can a fellow apply for an extension beyond two years? No, the fellowship is for a maximum of two years only.

Can fellows collaborate with other researchers at PRL? Yes, collaboration with other PRL researchers is encouraged.

Sources And References[Guidelines](https://www.prl.res.in/prl-eng/pdf)OkWas this helpful?
